description: Perform a spatial autocorrelation analysis of Sudden Infant Death Syndrome (SIDS) rates in North Carolina using the 'sf' and 'spdep' packages to identify geographical clusters from 1974. Using the 'nc.shp' dataset from the 'sf' package, calculate the SIDS Rate per 1,000 births (SID74/BIR74 * 1000). Conduct a Global Moran's I analysis using row-standardized Queen contiguity weights, saving the statistic and p-value to /home/ga/RProjects/output/global_moran.txt and a Moran scatterplot to /home/ga/RProjects/output/moran_scatterplot.png. Perform a Local Indicators of Spatial Association (LISA) analysis to identify significant hotspots and coldspots (p < 0.05). Save the results, including county name (NAME), SIDS Rate, Local Moran I, and p-value, to /home/ga/RProjects/output/nc_lisa_results.csv. Generate a cluster map and save it to /home/ga/RProjects/output/lisa_cluster_map.png. Write the analysis script to /home/ga/RProjects/sids_analysis.R.
